name: HCP-Aging / Aging Adult Brain Connectome (AABC)
description: A large adult-aging extension of the Human Connectome Project combining HCP-Aging (HCA) and the Aging Adult Brain Connectome (AABC) extension. Current AABC releases include cross-sectional and longitudinal 3T multimodal MRI sessions from typically aging adults ages 36-90+, with structural MRI, resting-state fMRI, task fMRI, diffusion MRI, ASL, high-resolution hippocampal T2 imaging, selected 7T MRS data, and broad non-imaging behavioral, cognitive, health, and biomeasure variables. It is a flagship resource for studying healthy brain aging, resilience, cognition, and age-related change in brain networks.
citation: Bookheimer SY, Salat DH, Terpstra M, et al. (2019). The Lifespan Human Connectome Project in Aging: An overview. NeuroImage, 185, 335-348.

publications.title: The Lifespan Human Connectome Project in Aging: An overview
